channel.title: AdvisoryOps — Healthcare Medical Device Advisories
channel.description: Healthcare and medical device cybersecurity advisories

item.title: #StopRansomware: Interlock
item.description: Summary Note : This joint Cybersecurity Advisory is part of an ongoing #StopRansomware effort to publish advisories for network defenders that detail various ransomware variants and ransomware threat actors. These #StopRansomware advisories include recently and historically observed tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs) and indicators of compromise (IOCs) to help organizations protect against ransomware. Visit stopransomware.gov to see all #StopRansomware advisories and to learn more about

item.title: CVE-2025-12699
item.description: The ZOLL ePCR IOS application reflects unsanitized user input into a WebView. Attacker-controlled strings placed into PCR fields (run number, incident, call sign, notes) are interpreted as HTML/JS when the app prints or renders that content. In the proof of concept (POC), injected scripts return local file content, which would allow arbitrary local file reads from the app's runtime context. These local files contain device and user data within the ePCR medical application, and if exposed, would

item.title: February 2026 CISO Brief: Privacy Deadlines, Clinical Impact, and Persistent Attack Paths
item.description: As healthcare organizations move closer to the February 16, 2026, compliance deadline for the updated 42 CFR Part 2 requirements, they are doing so in an environment defined by persistent ransomware activity, slow remediation of known exploited vulnerabilities, expanding clinical attack surfaces, and growing use of unmanaged technologies. This month’s Brief focuses on how these […]

item.title: CVE-2024-22774
item.description: An issue in Panoramic Corporation Digital Imaging Software v.9.1.2.7600 allows a local attacker to escalate privileges via the ccsservice.exe component.

item.title: CVE-2025-11266
item.description: An out-of-bounds write vulnerability exists in the Grassroots DICOM library (GDCM). The issue is triggered during parsing of a malformed DICOM file containing encapsulated PixelData fragments (compressed image data stored as multiple fragments). This vulnerability leads to a segmentation fault caused by an out-of-bounds memory access due to unsigned integer underflow in buffer indexing. It is exploitable via file input, simply opening a crafted malicious DICOM file is sufficient to trigger the c

item.title: CVE-2026-3650
item.description: A memory leak exists in the Grassroots DICOM library (GDCM). The bug occurs when parsing malformed DICOM files with non-standard VR types in file meta information. The vulnerability leads to vast memory allocations and resource depletion, triggering a denial-of-service condition. A maliciously crafted file can fill the heap in a single read operation without properly releasing it.

item.title: CVE-2024-12248
item.description: Contec Health CMS8000 Patient Monitor is vulnerable to an out-of-bounds write, which could allow an attacker to send specially formatted UDP requests in order to write arbitrary data. This could result in remote code execution.

item.title: CVE-2025-0683
item.description: In its default configuration, Contec Health CMS8000 Patient Monitor transmits plain-text patient data to a hard-coded public IP address when a patient is hooked up to the monitor. This could lead to a leakage of confidential patient data to any device with that IP address or an attacker in a machine-in-the-middle scenario.

item.title: CVE-2025-14346
item.description: WHILL Model C2 Electric Wheelchairs and Model F Power Chairs do not enforce authentication for Bluetooth connections. An attacker within range can pair with the device and issue movement commands, override speed restrictions, and manipulate configuration profiles without any credentials or user interaction.

item.title: Infusion Pump Software Correction: Fresenius Kabi Issues Correction for Ivenix Large Volume Pump Software
item.description: Fresenius Kabi is correcting Ivenix LVP software due to anomalies that could cause serious patient harm or death

item.title: Defibrillator, Automatic Implantable Cardioverter, With Cardiac Resynchronization (Crt-D) recall
item.description:
Two corrections to firmware due to Skipped-Charge Shock and Sensor Noise Anomaly are being made with one noninvasive upgrade. | Implantable Cardioverter Defibrilators
Epic DR/HF (V-233/V-337/V-338), Epic Plus DR/VR/HF (V-236/V-239/V-196/V-239T/V-196T/V-350)
Atlas DR (V242), and Atlas Plus DR/VR/HF (V-243/V-193C/V-340/V-341/V-343). | Device: Defibrillator, Automatic Implantable Cardioverter, With Cardiac Resynchronization (Crt-D).

item.title: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable) recall (Philips Medical Systems)
item.description: In Software versions below A.02.00, with the sync function enabled and the device switched from manual to AED, there may be an unexpected delay in delivery of energy | Philips Heartstart MRx Monitor/Defibrillator Models: M3535A (Hospital Use) , M3536A (EMS Use) | Philips Medical Systems | Device: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable).

item.title: BRICKSTORM Backdoor
item.description: Malware Analysis at a Glance Malware Name BRICKSTORM Original Publication Dec. 4, 2025 Last Update Feb. 11, 2026 Executive Summary The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), National Security Agency (NSA), and Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (Cyber Centre) assess People’s Republic of China (PRC) state-sponsored cyber actors are using BRICKSTORM malware for long-term persistence on victim systems. CISA, NSA, and Cyber Centre are releasing this Malware Analysis Report to share

item.title: Z-0096-2019: GE Healthcare Finland Oy
item.description: When multiple CARESCAPEMonitor B450 or B850 units are connected to the same network and a network overload occurs for a prolonged time, the monitors may simultaneously restart as designed. The monitor restart will not be completed until the network issue has been corrected. | GE Healthcare CARESCAPE Monitor B850 Product Usage: Intended uses of CARESCAPE B850 V2 in 510(k) countries [UR_CRSCP-1046]: The CARESCAPE Monitor B850 is a multi-parameter high acuity patient monitor intended for use

item.title: Z-0095-2019: GE Healthcare Finland Oy
item.description: When multiple CARESCAPEMonitor B450 or B850 units are connected to the same network and a network overload occurs for a prolonged time, the monitors may simultaneously restart as designed. The monitor restart will not be completed until the network issue has been corrected. | GE Healthcare CARESCAPE Monitor B450 Product Usage: Intended uses of CARESCAPE B450 in 510(k) countries [UR_CRSCP-885]: The CARESCAPE Monitor B450 is a multi-parameter patient monitor intended for use in multiple areas

item.title: Implantable Pacemaker Pulse-Generator recall (Cook Biotech, Inc.)
item.description: Adverse events- suspected pseudoaneurysm | Surgisis peripheral vascular patch; catalog number C-VSLH-4S-1x10. | Cook Biotech, Inc. | Device: Implantable Pacemaker Pulse-Generator.

item.title: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable) recall (Cardiac Science Corporation)
item.description: Some Powerheart AED Model 9300A may contain a suspect electronic component and there is a remote likelihood that the function of this device may be interrupted during the charge. | Powerheart AED G3 Automated External Defibrillator Model 9300A (Automatic). Cardiac Science AEDs are manufactured by: Manufacturing: Cardiac Science, Inc., 5474 Feltl Road, Minnetonka, MN 55343-7982. | Cardiac Science Corporation | Device: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable).

item.title: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable) recall (Heartsine Technologies Inc)
item.description: The affected Automatic External Defibrillators (AED) annouce a fault warning and shut themselves off before a shock can be delivered to the patient. This is reportedly due to slow capacitor charging rates which are interpreted by the AED software as a fault condition. | Heartsine Samaritan AED Defibrillators - Models: SAM 001, SAM 002, SAM 003. Serial numbers within range of 1270 - 2324 (not all are affected). | Heartsine Technologies Inc | Device: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearabl

item.title: Detector And Alarm, Arrhythmia recall (General Electric Medical Systems Information Technology)
item.description: Various user settings may change without an audible warning or visual indication. Examples of settings that may change are pacemaker detection which may turn from on to off, selected ECG leads for display, and selected gain settings for a selected waveform. | Solar 8000M Patient Monitor with software versions 3A, 3B and 3C | General Electric Medical Systems Information Technology | Device: Detector And Alarm, Arrhythmia.

item.title: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable) recall (Medtronic Emergency Response Systems, Inc.)
item.description: The LIFEPAK 12 biphasic defibrillator/ monitors may deliver inappropriate energy levels and fail to escalate energy levels when configured to do so. | LIFEPAK 12 defibrillator/ monitors biphasic | Medtronic Emergency Response Systems, Inc. | Device: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable).

item.title: Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (Non-Crt) recall (Biotronik Inc)
item.description: Potential for early depletion of Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator battery. | Belos VR Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD) with version A-K00.1.U/1 programmer software. | Biotronik Inc | Device: Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (Non-Crt).

item.title: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable) recall (Philips Medical Systems)
item.description: The device may disarm and not deliver shock when performing a Synchronized Cardioversion | Philips Medical HeartStart MRx Monitor/Defibrillator Model: M3535A, M3536A | Philips Medical Systems | Device: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable).

item.title: 16th March – Threat Intelligence Report
item.description: For the latest discoveries in cyber research for the week of 16th March, please download our Threat Intelligence Bulletin. TOP ATTACKS AND BREACHES United States-based medical technology company Stryker has suffered a cyberattack that caused a global disruption to its environment. The company said its surgical robotics, clinical communications platform, and life support monitors are […] The post 16th March – Threat Intelligence Report appeared first on Check Point Research .

item.title: Linux Kernel ksmbd Use-After-Free Remote Code Execution Vulnerability -
item.description: Linux Kernel ksmbd Use-After-Free Remote Code Execution Vulnerability -

item.title: Dialysis Tubing Set Correction: Vantive Updates Use Instructions for Prismaflex and Oxiris Sets
item.description: Prismaflex Set deaeration chambers may dislodge from the Prismaflex Control Unit during use.

item.title: Update: FDA Encourages the Public to Follow Established Choking Rescue Protocols - FDA Safety Communication
item.description: These choking rescue protocols include abdominal thrusts (also called the “Heimlich” maneuver) for children and adults but not anti-choking devices.

item.title: Insufflation Unit Recall: Olympus Removes High Flow Insufflation Units
item.description: Olympus is removing certain High Flow Insufflation Unit models due to software issues that may lead to overpressure events.

item.title: Electronic Homecare Bed Correction: Medline Updates Use Instructions for Homecare Beds
item.description: Medline updates use instructions for Electronic Homecare Beds due to risk of electrical fires and entrapment.

item.title: Sivem Pharmaceuticals Losartan HCT product recall (2021-10-13)
